techniques.evidence_quote: Developer accidentally merges company git repo to personal one (public) • Two AWS keys exposed
One of the AWS Keys used for initial reconnaissance • suspicious reconnaissance actions, such as: ListBuckets • GetCallerIdentity

attack_chain.description: A developer accidentally merged the company's internal git repository into a personal public GitHub repository, exposing two AWS access keys in the source code.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Developer accidentally merges company git repo to personal one (public) • Two AWS keys exposed

attack_chain.description: The threat actor obtained one of the exposed AWS access keys from the public GitHub repository (exact timing unknown) and used it to authenticate to AWS.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Key obtained (timing unknown) • One of the AWS Keys used for initial reconnaissance

attack_chain.description: The threat actor used the compromised AWS access key to perform initial reconnaissance against the AWS environment, including actions such as ListBuckets and GetCallerIdentity.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: One of the AWS Keys used for initial reconnaissance • suspicious reconnaissance actions, such as: ListBuckets • GetCallerIdentity

initial_access_vector: AWS access keys accidentally exposed in a public GitHub repository after a developer merged the company repository into their personal public repo.
persistence_mechanism: None observed; keys were blocked and rotated during containment before persistence was established.
privilege_escalation: None described in the source text.
